name: Mojave Plant Employees
description_short: Mojave Plant Employees Federal Credit Union is a manufacturing-focused credit union serving 200+ members since 1957 with $1.71M in assets and auto loan services.
description_long:
Mojave Plant Employees Federal Credit Union was established in 1957 and has operated continuously for over 65 years as a member-owned financial institution. Based in Mojave, California, the credit union serves a specific employee demographic with roots in the local manufacturing sector. With $1.71 million in total assets and more than 200 active members, it operates as a smaller, community-focused financial cooperative.

The credit union offers core banking services typical of member-owned institutions, with auto loans explicitly listed as an available product. Members can access banking services at their single branch location on Oak Creek Road. The organization accepts new membership applications beyond just current manufacturing employees—immediate family members of existing members are also eligible to join, broadening the potential member base slightly.

What distinguishes this credit union is its hyper-local focus and long operational history within the Mojave manufacturing community. As an employee-based credit union, it maintains a specific mission tied to serving workers in that sector rather than the general public. The institution has maintained continuous operations since the 1950s, indicating stability and established community relationships.

This is a very small credit union with limited service offerings and a single physical branch. The website provides minimal detail on deposit products, savings accounts, interest rates, fees, or other standard banking features. Prospective members should contact them directly to understand the full range of services available beyond auto loans. The small asset base and membership size mean fewer resources for technology, branch infrastructure, and service expansion compared to larger financial institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established in 1957 with 65+ years of continuous operation and community trust
Member-owned cooperative structure typically offers lower fees and better loan rates than traditional banks
Membership extended to immediate family of current employees, not just manufacturing workers
Auto loan services available as a core product offering
Local single-branch operation in Mojave serves the specific community it was founded for
Direct phone contact (661-823-3732) available for personalized service
cons: Very small institution with only $1.71M in assets limits product range and technological capabilities
Single branch location provides no geographic convenience outside Mojave area
Website provides minimal information on deposit products, rates, fees, or service details
Membership base of 200+ members is extremely small, limiting economies of scale
No online banking platform or services information visible, unclear what digital services exist
best_for: Current and former employees of Mojave manufacturing plants seeking member-owned banking
Immediate family members of existing credit union members eligible for membership
Local Mojave residents needing auto financing with a community-focused lender
diagnosis: Mojave Plant Employees Federal Credit Union is best suited for existing members, their families, and Mojave-area residents seeking auto loans from a small, locally-rooted institution. The main caveat is the extremely limited service information available and single-branch model—prospective members must contact them directly to understand what deposit products and full service suite they offer.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
